topic: Autonomous self-correcting AI agents for peer review validation and academic citation fraud detection
final_review:
The development of autonomous self-correcting AI agents for peer review validation and academic citation fraud detection has gained significant attention in recent years (Dwivedi et al., 2023; Cotton et al., 2023). The integration of artificial intelligence (AI) in higher education has been explored in various studies, highlighting its potential to enhance the learning experience (Olaf Zawacki‐Richter et al., 2019). However, the use of AI in academic settings also raises concerns about academic integrity, particularly with the emergence of generative conversational AI (Meyer et al., 2023).

Research on machine learning techniques has shown its effectiveness in various applications, including fluid mechanics (Brunton et al., 2019), supply chain management (Toorajipour et al., 2020), and cyber security (Shaukat et al., 2020). The application of machine learning in disease diagnosis has also been explored, demonstrating its potential to improve diagnosis accuracy (Ahsan et al., 2022). Furthermore, large language models have been reviewed, highlighting their architectures, applications, and challenges (Raiaan et al., 2024).

The use of AI in academic settings has sparked debates about its implications on academic integrity, with some studies highlighting the potential benefits of AI-generated content (Dwivedi et al., 2023), while others raise concerns about the risks of academic fraud (Cotton et al., 2023). Students' perceptions of generative AI in higher education have also been explored, revealing both positive and negative views (Chan et al., 2023). The emergence of deepfake technology has also been reviewed, highlighting its potential implications on academic integrity (Westerlund, 2019).

Despite the growing body of research on AI in academic settings, there are still gaps in our understanding of its implications on peer review validation and academic citation fraud detection. For instance, the use of AI in detecting citation fraud has not been extensively explored (Dwivedi et al., 2023). Moreover, the development of autonomous self-correcting AI agents for peer review validation requires further research (Meyer et al., 2023). The integration of AI in health sciences education has also been highlighted as an area of concern, with implications for critical thinking and academic integrity (Naqvi et al., 2025).

In conclusion, the development of autonomous self-correcting AI agents for peer review validation and academic citation fraud detection is a complex issue that requires further research. While AI has shown its potential in various applications, its implications on academic integrity and peer review validation need to be carefully considered (Jenkins, 2006; Dwivedi et al., 2020). Further studies are needed to address the research gaps and challenges associated with the use of AI in academic settings.

References:

Brunton, S. L., Noack, B. R., & Koumoutsakos, P. (2019). Machine Learning for Fluid Mechanics. Annual Review of Fluid Mechanics, 51, 577-604.

Cotton, D., Cotton, P. A., & Shipway, J. R. (2023). Chatting and cheating: Ensuring academic integrity in the era of ChatGPT. Journal of Academic Ethics, 1-12.
Dwivedi, Y. K., Ismagilova, E., & Hughes, D. L. (2020). Setting the future of digital and social media marketing research: Perspectives and research propositions. International Journal of Information Management, 50, 102168.
Dwivedi, Y. K., Kshetri, N., & Hughes, L. (2023). Opinion Paper: “So what if ChatGPT wrote it?” Multidisciplinary perspectives on opportunities, challenges and implications of generative conversational AI for research, practice and policy. International Journal of Information Management, 102642.
Jenkins, H. (2006). Confronting the Challenges of Participatory Culture: Media Education for the 21st Century.

Olaf Zawacki‐Richter, O., Marín, V. I., & Bond, M. (2019). Systematic review of research on artificial intelligence applications in higher education – where are the educators? International Journal of Educational Technology in Higher Education, 16(1), 1-15.

Shaukat, K., Luo, S., & Varadharajan, V. (2020). A Survey on Machine Learning Techniques for Cyber Security in the Last Decade. IEEE Access, 8, 3041951.
Toorajipour, R., Sohrabpour, V., & Nazarpour, A. (2020). Artificial intelligence in supply chain management: A systematic literature review. Journal of Business Research, 121, 102642.
